_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to conduct full statistical investigations — from asking a question to reporting our findings.

_source.w: We are learning to conduct full statistical investigations — from asking a question to reporting our findings.
y: Conducting a full investigation develops our ability to think systematically and draw evidence-based conclusions.
This process builds independence — we decide the question, method, display and analysis ourselves.
Full investigations prepare us for research in science, humanities and the real world.
eg: Investigating: 'Is there a relationship between the amount of sleep Year 6 students get and their concentration in class?'
Designing a fair survey, collecting data from a representative sample and choosing an appropriate display.
Analysing our data using mode, range and shape of distribution to draw conclusions.
Writing a report that includes our question, method, results and conclusion.
Discussing the limitations of our investigation and suggesting improvements.
Presenting our findings to the class using graphs, statistics and clear explanations.
